name: rad-planner
description:
Plan a project before you write code — or replan after a pivot, deepen an existing plan, or check for drift between plan and reality. rad-planner walks you through a five-phase planning conversation (Constitution → Scope → Sequence → Quality Gates → Doc-Set Recommendation), then writes the approved doc set as the plan's first executed milestone. The plan is the deliverable; the docs follow it.

Four entry points cover the lifecycle:
- `--full` — greenfield plan from scratch
- `--improve` — deepen or continue an existing plan
- `--drift` — compare reality to the plan
- `--pivot` — substantial re-plan with disposition manifest

A read-only `--assessment` mode answers "where does this project stand?" without writing anything. `--auto` produces DRAFT-labeled strawmen for unattended runs and does not write ADRs (candidate decisions land in `docs/planning/proposed-decisions.md` for review).

Two project-story skills (`/project-story`, `/refresh-story`) turn the canonical doc set into plain-English narrative for non-developers, collaborators, or your future self. Four mechanical validators back the workflow: `plan-lint.py`, `status-validator.py`, `doc-redundancy.py`, `doc-contradiction.py`.

Aligned with published OpenAI/Anthropic project-structure research: three-band memory model (operating manual / project memory / execution state), intent-vs-reality split between `planning/current.md` and `status.md`, CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md as conditional operating manuals based on agent scope. Pairs with rad-session for the full lifecycle; works standalone.
